prose: Fukui reactivity indices were computed for furan (9 atoms: O, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) using the fukui-reactivity skill. Method: DFT, tier 'standard' — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states (cation and anion) were computed as doublets (charge ±1, mult 2). Partial charges use the Mulliken scheme. The geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file without re-optimization at this level. All three SCF states converged; integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true). No warnings reported. The most electrophilic and most nucleophilic site is C5 (atom 5, 1-based), with f+ = 1.9242931 (headline value) and f- = 0.1616371. The α-carbons (C4, C5) have the largest positive dual descriptors (~1.762), consistent with furan's known preference for electrophilic aromatic substitution at the α-position. The oxygen atom (f+ = -1.33162, dual = -1.41544) is the dominant nucleophilic/lone-pair donor site. Charge conservation: sum(f+) = sum(f-) = 1.0000. Output plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260703-194013_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
